mission.title: kubernetes: managedFields options are very verbose, making kubectl usage a bit
mission.description: managedFields options are very verbose, making kubectl usage a bit . Requested by 300+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubernetes deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubernetes version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubernetes
helm list -n kubernetes 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubernetes installation.

steps.title: Review kubernetes configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubernetes configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubernetes
kubectl get configmap -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubernetes
```
Recently (1.18?) a new field was added to configs, managedFields. This is incredibly verbose, which makes kubectl commands like `-oyaml` and `edit` painful.

steps.title: Apply the fix for managedFields options are very verbose, making kubectl…
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubernetes -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubernetes
kubectl get events -n kubernetes --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "managedFields options are very verbose, making kubectl…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: With the introduction of `.meta.managedFields` it's cumbersome to edit any resource due to the size of that additional fields. This PR hides those fields, since they should not even be modified by consumers directly.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubernetes installed or the issue environment reproducible.
